title: The Possum

jokes.text: Valentine's Day was a month ago. / Why are you giving it to me now? / Whatever. Happy early Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Character gives belated Valentine's gift, gets called out on timing
jokes.punchline: Instead of acknowledging the mistake, pivots to calling it an early gift
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection that shows character's inability to admit error. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: This is one of those nanny-cam teddy bears, isn't it? / What? No. It's a regular, camera-less teddy bear.
jokes.setup: Ann correctly identifies the surveillance teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Tom's terrible lie using the obviously made-up term 'camera-less'
jokes.explanation: Tom's characteristically bad lying with the absurd 'camera-less' descriptor. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Just put it in your bedroom, don't even think about it. It's a robot bear. It's programmed to snuggle!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to get Ann to place the nanny-cam bear
jokes.punchline: Escalates to creepy 'robot bear programmed to snuggle' excuse
jokes.explanation: Tom's desperation leading to increasingly inappropriate suggestions. Character-specific creepiness.

jokes.text: I'll take it. / Donna, there's a camera in it. / I know.
jokes.setup: Tom warns Donna about the hidden camera
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact 'I know' - she wants the surveillance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna moment - subverts expectation with her confident acceptance of being watched. Maximum economy with huge character payoff.

jokes.text: Can I read your mail? / No. Please, don't. / Fine.
jokes.setup: April asks to read Ann's mail while house-sitting
jokes.punchline: April's casual 'Fine' suggests she'll do it anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic April - asking inappropriate questions with complete nonchalance. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Don't let Tom make a copy. / That's right. Good.
jokes.setup: Ann gives specific instruction about Tom and keys
jokes.punchline: April already knows this rule, suggesting Tom's reputation for key-copying
jokes.explanation: Establishes Tom's creepy pattern behavior through others' awareness of it.

jokes.text: I've seen the way she takes care of her house.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining why she didn't ask Leslie to house-sit
jokes.punchline: Subtle dig at Leslie's messy lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Mild character observation. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I don't like Ann. / Become friends. Who knows?
jokes.setup: Ann hopes getting closer to April might lead to friendship
jokes.punchline: Cut to April flatly stating 'I don't like Ann'
jokes.explanation: Perfect mockumentary editing joke. The timing and contrast between Ann's hope and April's bluntness is expertly executed.

jokes.text: I've always dreamed of you. / Excuse me? / Meeting you.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to say she's dreamed of meeting Evelyn
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'dreamed of you' creating awkward sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Standard verbal stumble creating awkward moment. Well-executed but not original.

jokes.text: Fairway Frank is this awful possum who lives near the sixth hole... he's actually number three on the Parks Department's Most Wanted Pest List
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining about the troublesome possum
jokes.punchline: Parks Department has an official 'Most Wanted Pest List' with rankings
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist bureaucracy joke. Leslie would definitely maintain an official pest ranking system.

jokes.text: right behind the bats, who like to poop on the bell tower, and Poopy, the raccoon who poops all over the high school cafeteria
jokes.setup: Continuing the pest list explanation
jokes.punchline: The raccoon is literally named 'Poopy' and specializes in cafeteria defecation
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation with the named raccoon. The poop focus is juvenile but fits the absurd bureaucracy.

jokes.text: Let's be honest. Animal Control is not the most effective branch of our government. They're a bunch of burned-out morons.
jokes.setup: Evelyn suggests calling Animal Control
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment of Animal Control's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Standard government incompetence joke. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Ma'am, the next time we speak, we shall be dancing on the grave of a possum.
jokes.setup: Leslie accepting the possum-catching mission
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic promise to literally dance on the possum's grave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie enthusiasm applied to a ridiculous task. The grave-dancing imagery is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Right this way is the exit.
jokes.setup: Ron is supposed to show Evelyn around
jokes.punchline: Instead immediately directs her to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - maximum efficiency in getting rid of government visitor. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Yo, police! / No, you're not. Coffee!
jokes.setup: Andy greets April like she's police
jokes.punchline: April corrects him - she's bringing coffee, not law enforcement
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's confusion and April's directness. Simple character interaction.

jokes.text: I only ever bring you coffee. / And it is my very favorite non-alcoholic hot drink, except for hot tea. And hot orange juice, weirdly delicious.
jokes.setup: April points out she always brings coffee
jokes.punchline: Andy's bizarre ranking system including 'hot orange juice'
jokes.explanation: Andy's weird food preferences showing his childlike nature. Hot orange juice is particularly odd.

jokes.text: I got a really bad case of shoeshine head today.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he needs coffee
jokes.punchline: References made-up condition 'shoeshine head'
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to make up medical conditions. Sets up the talking head explanation.

jokes.text: It's when you shine too many shoes and the fumes create a thunderstorm in your brain.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his made-up condition
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation involving brain thunderstorms
jokes.explanation: 'Thunderstorm in your brain' is a brilliantly absurd medical description. Very Andy-specific pseudo-science.

jokes.text: Cures include coffee, cheeseburgers
jokes.setup: Continuing the medical explanation
jokes.punchline: Treatment is just things Andy likes
jokes.explanation: The 'cures' being regular food shows Andy's self-serving medical theories.

jokes.text: and napping on the floor.
jokes.setup: Final cure for shoeshine head
jokes.punchline: Floor napping as legitimate medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Floor napping as medicine perfectly captures Andy's childlike logic and laziness.

jokes.text: You don't have any code violations, do you? / [Long pause] / Nope.
jokes.setup: Mark asks if Ron has code violations
jokes.punchline: Ron's suspicious pause before obviously lying
jokes.explanation: The pause does all the comedic work - we know Ron's workshop is a disaster. Great use of timing.

jokes.text: We'll get to it first thing Monday. / Today's Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Animal Control promises to handle the possum Monday
jokes.punchline: It's already Wednesday, showing their incompetence
jokes.explanation: Demonstrates Animal Control's laziness efficiently. Leslie's callback confirms their incompetence.

jokes.text: We're acting under direct orders from Mayor Gunderson's dog.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to motivate Animal Control
jokes.punchline: Claims authority comes from the mayor's dog
jokes.explanation: Absurd authority claim that's technically true - the dog was bitten. Leslie's creative bureaucratic logic.

jokes.text: That would be Harris and Brett. / But they're not here. / Isn't that them, there? / Nope.
jokes.setup: Eugene identifies his best guys who aren't present
jokes.punchline: They're clearly visible but he denies it
jokes.explanation: Classic government worker avoidance behavior. Visual contradiction makes it work.

jokes.text: Want your ball back? Come with me. / Oh, man! / How you know my name, homie?
jokes.setup: Leslie uses golf ball as bait for Brett
jokes.punchline: Brett thinks she literally knows his name is 'Homie'
jokes.explanation: Brett's confusion about casual slang being his actual name shows his dim nature.

jokes.text: I used to love Tiger Woods because he was a great champion. But after that sex scandal? The man is a god!
jokes.setup: Tom sets up what sounds like moral disappointment about Tiger Woods
jokes.punchline: Reveals the scandal actually increased his admiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection revealing Tom's skewed values. The reversal is well-structured and character-specific.

jokes.text: You are the first non-me to set foot in this building in 10 years.
jokes.setup: Ron welcoming Mark to his workshop
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing 'first non-me' instead of 'first person other than me'
jokes.explanation: Ron's characteristic awkward phrasing when trying to be formal. Shows his social limitations.

jokes.text: Sure it is. It's up to the Swanson Code.
jokes.setup: Mark says the workshop isn't up to code
jokes.punchline: Ron claims it meets his personal 'Swanson Code'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron libertarian philosophy - creating his own standards. 'Swanson Code' is quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: It's the same liberty that gives me the right to fart in my own car. Are you gonna tell a man that he can't fart in his own car?
jokes.setup: Ron defending his right to ignore safety codes
jokes.punchline: Compares it to the fundamental right to fart in one's own car
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from safety codes to fundamental American freedoms via car farting. Very Ron logic.

jokes.text: This says it should be recharged June of 1996. / Those dates are arbitrary. They're like those expiration dates that the government forces companies to put on yogurt and medicine.
jokes.setup: Mark points out the fire extinguisher expired 14+ years ago
jokes.punchline: Ron dismisses safety dates as government conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Ron's anti-government paranoia applied to basic safety. Compares fire safety to yogurt dates.

jokes.text: How long do you think it would take me to learn golf, Leslie? / I could teach you... / Yeah, I don't want to do all that. I think I just want some of those dope pants.
jokes.setup: Tom seems interested in learning golf, Leslie offers to teach
jokes.punchline: Tom only wants golf for the fashion, not the sport
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - superficial interest in status symbols over substance. 'Dope pants' is very Tom.

jokes.text: Move! Get out of the way!
jokes.setup: Leslie wants everyone to move slowly around the possum
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately panics and runs
jokes.explanation: Tom's cowardice undermining the plan. Predictable but fits his character.

jokes.text: Come on, Brendanawicz, relax. Let me make you a canoe.
jokes.setup: Tension over code violations
jokes.punchline: Ron offers to make Mark a canoe as if that solves the problem
jokes.explanation: Ron's solution to bureaucratic conflict is handcrafted woodworking. Very character-specific problem-solving.

jokes.text: Andy will come in to... / Andy! / I got it!
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining the careful plan
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately ignores it and tackles the possum
jokes.explanation: Andy's impulsiveness ruining Leslie's careful planning. Physical comedy with character consistency.

jokes.text: You're next, Poopy.
jokes.setup: Successful capture of Fairway Frank
jokes.punchline: Leslie threatens the poop-obsessed raccoon from earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the pest list. Shows Leslie's expanding war on animal crime.

jokes.text: Please, my friends call me Andy Radical. / No, we don't.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to introduce his cool nickname
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Andy's delusions of coolness vs reality. Leslie's quick shutdown is perfect timing.

jokes.text: By day, Andy Dwyer, shoeshine-ist. By a different time of day, Andy Radical, possum tackler. And by night, I do whatever I want. No job.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his multiple identities
jokes.punchline: 'By night' identity is just unemployment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure building up to anticlimatic 'No job.' The awkward 'different time of day' phrasing is perfect Andy.

jokes.text: How about an extra recycling can? No. Laminated bus pass? No. / What time do you usually go to sleep? Because my best ideas usually come to me at night.
jokes.setup: Leslie rapidly firing off favor requests
jokes.punchline: Wants to call Evelyn at home with late-night ideas
jokes.explanation: Leslie's overwhelming enthusiasm and boundary issues. The escalation to personal sleep schedules is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Am I sure the possum we caught is Fairway Frank? Yes. Am I quite sure? No. Am I sure enough? Maybe.
jokes.setup: Leslie questioning her certainty
jokes.punchline: Each question reveals decreasing confidence
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure showing Leslie's growing doubt. The question format builds nicely to uncertainty.

jokes.text: As a city official, it's important that I ask myself a lot of questions. Does doing so help me make decisions? Uh...
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her decision-making process
jokes.punchline: Can't even answer whether her process works
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Leslie's over-analysis leading to paralysis. Self-aware and character-specific.

jokes.text: And then afterwards, had sex with Mark and everyone talked about it.
jokes.setup: Andy recalling reporter Shauna's previous article
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions her affair with Mark in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Andy's social obliviousness creating awkward moment. His blunt reference to workplace gossip.

jokes.text: So, I thought to myself, 'Don't think, Andy, act.' / So, you weren't thinking? / Not at all. I cannot emphasize enough how little I was thinking.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his heroic mindset
jokes.punchline: Proudly emphasizes his complete lack of thought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - turning thoughtlessness into a virtue. The escalating emphasis on not thinking is brilliantly character-specific.

jokes.text: You know what? 'Friends' sounds stupid. Colleagues.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his relationship with pit vermin
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to the more professional 'colleagues'
jokes.explanation: Andy's attempt at sophistication by upgrading vermin from 'friends' to 'colleagues.' Shows his confused social awareness.

jokes.text: They are bad at sharing, but they are good at tag.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his vermin colleagues
jokes.punchline: Reviews them like coworkers with specific skills
jokes.explanation: Treating vermin like workplace associates with performance reviews. The specific skills assessment is absurdly detailed.

jokes.text: I broke up with Ann. Yeah. Shortly after she kicked me out and told me we wouldn't be together anymore.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his breakup with Ann
jokes.punchline: Claims he broke up with her after she had already dumped him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy self-delusion. The contradiction within one sentence shows his denial and face-saving attempts.

jokes.text: Of all the things I thought would bring us back together, catching a possum was never on that list.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing the article might help win Ann back
jokes.punchline: Possum-catching wasn't in his relationship repair strategy
jokes.explanation: Andy's genuine surprise at this romantic angle. Implies he did have a list of reunion strategies.

jokes.text: Seriously, man, when you wear these clothes, you just feel better than everyone else. You know? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining golf attire
jokes.punchline: Openly admits clothing makes him feel superior to others
jokes.explanation: Tom's shallow materialism stated plainly. His honesty about feeling superior is both refreshing and revealing.

jokes.text: Ooh. Tommy needs a banana. You guys good?
jokes.setup: Tense conversation between Ron and Mark
jokes.punchline: Tom interrupts with random banana announcement
jokes.explanation: Tom's self-absorbed interruption during conflict. Third-person self-reference adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Ooh. Silly me. There was this little pom-pom on my glove and it fell off. Have you seen it? Could you help me look for it real quick?
jokes.setup: Tom trying to distract from Ron's argument
jokes.punchline: Obvious fake search for nonexistent pom-pom
jokes.explanation: Weak distraction attempt. The pom-pom detail is specific but the overall execution is clumsy.

jokes.text: Fairway Frank / You're gonna die / You're gonna fry, oh, yeah / You guilty son of a bitch / You're gonna fry / When they flip that switch
jokes.setup: Tom creating a song about the possum
jokes.punchline: Death penalty song for a possum with electric chair references
jokes.explanation: Tom's dramatic approach to pest control. The electric chair reference for a possum is absurdly disproportionate.

jokes.text: Hypothetically, if you were going on a mission to, say, catch a guilty whale...
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to discuss the possum situation indirectly
jokes.punchline: Creates bizarre whale metaphor instead of just talking about possums
jokes.explanation: Leslie's overthinking leads to unnecessarily complex metaphor. Shows her tendency to overcompcilate.

jokes.text: Not because I'm pissed at Andy, which I'm not. Because I care so deeply about possums. / Because they're so adorable.
jokes.setup: Mark claiming he's not motivated by jealousy
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan delivery suggesting possums are adorable
jokes.explanation: April's sarcastic support of Mark's transparent jealousy. Her deadpan delivery of 'adorable' for possums.

jokes.text: I think I nailed it. I fell off the stool once when I was trying to look serious, but I'm okay.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Considers falling off stool part of 'nailing it'
jokes.explanation: Andy's delusional confidence despite physical mishap. His assessment that he 'nailed it' while falling.

jokes.text: Man, our happy reactions are super different.
jokes.setup: April gives minimal reaction to Andy's excitement
jokes.punchline: Andy notices but misinterprets her complete lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Andy's obliviousness to April's sarcasm. He thinks they're both happy, just expressing differently.

jokes.text: Mr. Campopiano, those are photos of three different possums. / Huh.
jokes.setup: Golf course worker supposedly identifying Fairway Frank
jokes.punchline: Can't tell possums apart, responds with casual 'Huh'
jokes.explanation: Reveals the 'expert' can't identify the pest. His casual reaction to being wrong.

jokes.text: Can you do that, April? April, can you do that? Can you get the possum out of here? Can you sneak it out of here? April, can you do it? Can you do it? Please, April, tell me you can do it!
jokes.setup: Leslie needs April's help with the possum
jokes.punchline: Escalating desperation with repetitive pleading
jokes.explanation: Leslie's panic leading to repetitive begging. The escalation works but could be more economical.

jokes.text: I can't kill the possum, because it might be innocent. I can't let the possum go, because it might be guilty. I can't make a good soup, can't do a handstand in a pool, can't spell the word 'lieutenant.'
jokes.setup: Leslie's moral dilemma about the possum
jokes.punchline: Spirals into unrelated personal inadequacies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from moral crisis to random life failures. The 'lieutenant' spelling is specific and relatable.

jokes.text: Help! Help, my arm looks like it's bleeding! / Holy cow! Leslie! / Oh, boy. Oh, look how much it's bleeding. Everybody look at it. Everybody look over here!
jokes.setup: Leslie creating a distraction
jokes.punchline: Overdramatic fake injury performance
jokes.explanation: Leslie's bad acting creating obvious distraction. The performance is intentionally over-the-top.

jokes.text: It's ketchup! / Is it? / Jerry. Jerry. Yes. It's ketchup.
jokes.setup: Someone discovers Leslie's fake blood
jokes.punchline: Jerry's uncertain confirmation even when directly asked
jokes.explanation: Jerry's characteristic uncertainty even about obvious facts. Leslie's manipulation exposed.

jokes.text: Baby meerkats do not start foraging for food until about one month old.
jokes.setup: April standing guard over the possum
jokes.punchline: Random meerkat fact as guard duty commentary
jokes.explanation: April's random animal facts show her boredom and odd interests. Completely unrelated to the situation.

jokes.text: Does he want to have sex with a dead possum? / No! No. He's not a monster.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking what the mayor wants to do with the possum
jokes.punchline: Leslie's mind goes to disturbing place, Evelyn horrified
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that shows Leslie's overactive imagination. Evelyn's horrified denial.

jokes.text: He wants to stuff it and hang it above the urinal in his office bathroom so that little flecks of pee can get on it forever.
jokes.setup: Explaining what the mayor actually wants
jokes.punchline: Grotesque trophy plan involving permanent urine exposure
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but creative revenge fantasy. The 'little flecks of pee forever' is viscerally gross.

jokes.text: And I will not reveal his location, no matter how much you ask me. But I'm going to stop talking now because I'm afraid I may accidentally say where it is, so please go.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she won't reveal the possum's location
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits she might accidentally reveal it
jokes.explanation: Leslie's inability to keep secrets even from herself. Self-aware about her own weakness.

jokes.text: But security footage later revealed that it was actually a goose. / That's great. / No, it's awful, Tom. How would you feel if you killed an innocent duck and let a vicious goose waddle free?
jokes.setup: Tom thinks the duck/goose mix-up is good news
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic concern for waterfowl justice
jokes.explanation: Leslie's moral outrage over bird misidentification. 'Vicious goose waddle free' is great phrasing.

jokes.text: The possum got loose at Ann's house.
jokes.setup: April calls for help, asks Tom not to tell Leslie
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately tells Leslie
jokes.explanation: Tom's immediate betrayal of April's confidence. Classic sitcom information management failure.

jokes.text: It must love tacky pictures of flowers.
jokes.setup: Possum won't leave Ann's house
jokes.punchline: April's dig at Ann's decor
jokes.explanation: April taking opportunity to insult Ann's taste even during crisis. Very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Those city planning guys can be real pains in the ass. / Okay. I just want you to know that I still don't think city codes... / Ron, shut up.
jokes.setup: Andy offers to help Ron despite their disagreement
jokes.punchline: Mark tells Ron to shut up but in a friendly way
jokes.explanation: Friendship overcoming professional disagreement. Mark's 'shut up' is affectionate rather than hostile.

jokes.text: And if the Swanson Code happens to overlap with the City Government Code... / Shut up.
jokes.setup: Ron still trying to justify his approach
jokes.punchline: Mark's repeated friendly 'shut up'
jokes.explanation: Ron's continued stubbornness met with Mark's patient friendship. Callback to the Swanson Code.

jokes.text: What if it laid eggs in the bed?
jokes.setup: Leslie worried about possum damage
jokes.punchline: Doesn't understand possum biology
jokes.explanation: Leslie's panic leading to biological ignorance. Possums don't lay eggs, showing her stress.

jokes.text: He's gonna be pissed at me. I already ruined his big day, and it's my fault that it's in Ann's house, who he's obviously still in love with.
jokes.setup: Leslie not wanting to call Andy for help
jokes.punchline: Recognizes Andy's obvious feelings for Ann
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awareness of the love triangle dynamics. Shows her emotional intelligence about others.

jokes.text: Oh. April. / He'll forgive you. / You think so? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: April worried about Andy being mad at her
jokes.punchline: Leslie's gentle reassurance to April
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Leslie's nurturing side. More heartwarming than comedic.

jokes.text: And, April, any time you want to talk about boys... / Oh, my God! Stop!
jokes.setup: Leslie offering to be April's confidante
jokes.punchline: April's horrified rejection of girl talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Leslie's maternal instincts and April's anti-social nature.

jokes.text: 'I wouldn't have been able to do it without an early-morning caffeine boost from the amazing April Ludgate. She gave me the liquid courage I needed to wrestle that beast to the ground.'
jokes.setup: Andy giving April credit in newspaper
jokes.punchline: 'Liquid courage' for coffee and calling possum a 'beast'
jokes.explanation: Andy's dramatic language elevating coffee to heroic fuel and possum to monster. Sweet gesture to April.

jokes.text: And I want to be wearing a huge, beautiful, blue hat!
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her future distinguished legacy
jokes.punchline: Specific focus on wearing a blue hat at the gala
jokes.explanation: Leslie's grand political vision reduced to fashion concerns. The specificity of 'huge, beautiful, blue hat' is perfect.

jokes.text: Possum. There was a possum. We captured a possum and we brought it into your house and it got out and it might have laid eggs in your bed.
jokes.setup: Ann asking if there were any problems
jokes.punchline: April's blunt, escalating confession
jokes.explanation: April's characteristically blunt honesty creating maximum awkwardness. Includes the egg misconception.

jokes.text: And it went into your laundry and your kitchen, and it touched all your bras and I'm so sorry. It's our fault.
jokes.setup: April continuing her confession
jokes.punchline: Specific mention of bra contamination
jokes.explanation: The bra detail makes the violation more personal and gross. April's genuine remorse showing through.

jokes.text: April, run! / Run, April. Sorry, Ann! I love you!
jokes.setup: Ann processing the possum revelation
jokes.punchline: Leslie and April fleeing, April's random 'I love you' to Ann
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom escape scene. April's unexpected declaration of love while fleeing is perfectly absurd.

tmdb_overview: When a possum bites the mayor's dog on a golf course, Leslie is asked to form a task force to catch the pesky animal. Meanwhile, Ron is upset when Mark refuses to bend the rules for him.
insight: Absurdist escalation hits 77 jokes in 23 minutes, but impact stays below Parks' season average.
